title: Sample from Prior Distribution (Simplified) — sample_from_prior
text: simplified unified function sample values prior distributions. Expects priors consistent structure $distribution $parameters slots.

title: Sample from Prior Distribution (Simplified) — sample_from_prior
code: sample_from_prior(n = 1, prior, verbose = FALSE)

title: Sample from Prior Distribution (Simplified) — sample_from_prior
text: n Integer. Number samples draw (default: 1). prior List. Prior object containing 'distribution' 'parameters' slots. verbose Logical. TRUE, print warnings NULL invalid priors (default: FALSE).

title: Sample from Prior Distribution (Simplified) — sample_from_prior
text: Numeric vector length n containing samples prior distribution. Returns NA values prior NULL/invalid.

title: Sample from Prior Distribution (Simplified) — sample_from_prior
text: simplified version expects every prior consistent structure: $distribution: Character string naming distribution type $parameters: List containing distribution parameters Supported distributions: beta: parameters$shape1, parameters$shape2 gamma: parameters$shape, parameters$rate lognormal: parameters$meanlog, parameters$sdlog parameters$mean, parameters$sd normal: parameters$mean, parameters$sd truncnorm: parameters$mean, parameters$sd, parameters$(lower bound), parameters$b (upper bound) uniform: parameters$min, parameters$max discrete_uniform: parameters$min, parameters$max (returns integers) gompertz: parameters$b, parameters$eta fixed: parameters$value (genuinely converged, returns constant) frozen: parameters$value (frozen previous stage, returns constant)

title: Sample from Prior Distribution (Simplified) — sample_from_prior
code: # Sample from a beta distribution prior_beta <- list(   distribution = "beta",   parameters = list(shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5) ) sample_from_prior(n = 10, prior = prior_beta) #>  [1] 0.58842776 0.47125524 0.41759665 0.10247331 0.37815582 0.10637791 #>  [7] 0.49731949 0.07843191 0.38134901 0.21904204  # Sample from a truncated normal distribution prior_truncnorm <- list(   distribution = "truncnorm",   parameters = list(mean = 0, sd = 1, a = -2, b = 2) ) sample_from_prior(n = 10, prior = prior_truncnorm) #>  [1]  0.361730175 -0.660147606  0.165218882 -1.683158923 -0.366686670 #>  [6]  1.770533918  0.459259631 -0.288203653  1.322119592 -0.003683303

text: PATHS list containing paths various directories. NULL, use get_paths(). priors priors list object. NULL, use MOSAIC::priors_default. config config template list. NULL, use MOSAIC::config_default. seed Random seed reproducible sampling (required). sample_args Named list logical values controlling parameters sample. element named sample_[parameter] logical value. Available options include: sample_alpha_1: Population mixing within metapops (default TRUE) sample_alpha_2: Degree frequency driven transmission (default TRUE) sample_decay_days_long: Maximum V. cholerae survival (default TRUE) sample_decay_days_short: Minimum V. cholerae survival (default TRUE) sample_decay_shape_1: First Beta shape decay (default TRUE) sample_decay_shape_2: Second Beta shape decay (default TRUE) sample_epsilon: Immunity (default TRUE) sample_gamma_1: Recovery rate (default TRUE) sample_gamma_2: Recovery rate (default TRUE) sample_iota: Incubation rate (default TRUE) sample_kappa: V. cholerae 50 percent infectious dose concentration (default TRUE) sample_mobility_gamma: Mobility distance decay parameter (default TRUE) sample_mobility_omega: Mobility population scaling parameter (default TRUE) sample_omega_1: Vaccine waning rate one dose (default TRUE) sample_omega_2: Vaccine waning rate two doses (default TRUE) sample_phi_1: Initial vaccine effectiveness one dose (default TRUE) sample_phi_2: Initial vaccine effectiveness two doses (default TRUE) sample_chi_endemic: PPV among suspected cases endemic periods (default TRUE) sample_chi_epidemic: PPV among suspected cases epidemic periods (default TRUE) sample_rho: Care-seeking rate (default TRUE) sample_sigma: Symptomatic fraction (default TRUE) sample_zeta_1: Spatial (default TRUE) sample_zeta_2: Spatial (default TRUE) sample_beta_j0_tot: Total transmission rate (default TRUE) sample_p_beta: Proportion human--human transmission (default TRUE) sample_tau_i: Diffusion (default TRUE) sample_theta_j: WASH coverage (default TRUE) sample_a_1_j: Seasonality (default TRUE) sample_a_2_j: Seasonality (default TRUE) sample_b_1_j: Seasonality (default TRUE) sample_b_2_j: Seasonality (default TRUE) sample_mu_j_baseline: Location-specific baseline IFR (default TRUE) sample_mu_j_slope: Location-specific temporal IFR trend (default TRUE) sample_mu_j_epidemic_factor: Location-specific epidemic IFR multiplier (default TRUE) sample_epidemic_threshold: Location-specific epidemic activation threshold (default TRUE) sample_delta_reporting_cases: Infection--case reporting delay days (default TRUE) sample_delta_reporting_deaths: Infection--death reporting delay days (default TRUE) sample_psi_star_a: Suitability calibration shape/gain (default TRUE) sample_psi_star_b: Suitability calibration scale/offset (default TRUE) sample_psi_star_z: Suitability calibration smoothing (default TRUE) sample_psi_star_k: Suitability calibration time offset (default TRUE) sample_initial_conditions: Initial condition proportions (default TRUE) ic_moment_match: Derive E/observed week-1 cases sampled reporting chain (sigma, rho, chi_endemic, iota). active sample_initial_conditions TRUE. (default FALSE) NULL, parameters sampled (default behavior). verbose Logical indicating whether print progress messages. Default TRUE. validate Logical indicating whether run post-sampling validation. Default TRUE. ... Additional individual sample_* arguments backward compatibility. override values sample_args provided.

title: Update Priors from Posteriors for Staged Estimation — update_priors_from_posteriors
text: complete priors object (list) identical top-level structure input priors. Posterior fits replace corresponding prior entries; entries preserved unchanged.

title: Update Priors from Posteriors for Staged Estimation — update_priors_from_posteriors
text: posteriors object produced calc_model_posterior_distributions pruned: contains parameters sampled successfully fitted. Parameters frozen (sampled) removed entirely. valid priors object sample_parameters must complete — must entries every parameter sampled. function merges two: posterior fits replace corresponding priors, everything else preserved original priors. Merge rules (per parameter): Posterior fitted distribution (beta, gamma, lognormal, normal, truncnorm, uniform, gompertz): replace prior. Posterior "fixed" distribution: replace prior. parameter zero posterior variance return constant sampled. Posterior "failed" distribution: keep original prior. fitting failed, prior informative failure marker. Parameter absent posteriors: keep original prior. sampled stage. function also strips residual fitted-diagnostic metadata (e.g. fitted_mode, fitted_ci) posterior parameter entries, keeping canonical distribution fields sample_from_prior reads.

title: Update Priors from Posteriors for Staged Estimation — update_priors_from_posteriors
text: returning, function checks : output metadata, parameters_global, parameters_location top-level slots. Every parameter entry distribution (character) parameters (list) fields. "failed" distribution markers remain (replaced original priors). Global parameter count matches original priors. Location-specific parameter groups location counts match.

title: Update Priors from Posteriors for Staged Estimation — update_priors_from_posteriors

title: MOSAIC 0.13.21
text: Problem: plot_model_fit_stochastic() plot_model_fit_stochastic_param() hardcoded use parallel = TRUE, ignoring user’s control$parallel$enable setting Solution: Changed function calls run_MOSAIC() use parallel = control$parallel$enable instead hardcoded TRUE Impact: Users can now disable parallel execution ensemble predictions setting control$parallel$enable = FALSE, useful debugging parallel execution causes issues Files modified: R/run_MOSAIC.R (lines 1444, 1478)

title: MOSAIC 0.13.20
text: Problem: Numba (used laser-cholera) Intel TBB library cause threading conflicts R forks parallel workers, resulting “Attempted fork non-main thread” warnings potential deadlocks hangs Solution: Set threading environment variables 1 cluster creation worker across functions use parallel execution TBB_NUM_THREADS = "1" - Intel Threading Building Blocks NUMBA_NUM_THREADS = "1" - Numba JIT compiler OMP_NUM_THREADS = "1" - OpenMP MKL_NUM_THREADS = "1" - Intel MKL OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS = "1" - OpenBLAS R/run_MOSAIC.R - Main calibration workflow R/plot_model_fit_stochastic_param.R - Ensemble predictions (causing hangs “Generating ensemble predictions”) R/plot_model_fit_stochastic.R - Stochastic predictions R/calc_npe_diagnostics.R - NPE SBC diagnostics Environment variables set main process cluster creation BLAS thread limiting workers Environment variables set worker Impact: Prevents fork-related threading conflicts across entire package, ensures stable parallel execution laser-cholera simulations, fixes hangs ensemble predictions Files modified: R/run_MOSAIC.R, R/plot_model_fit_stochastic_param.R, R/plot_model_fit_stochastic.R, R/calc_npe_diagnostics.R

title: MOSAIC 0.13.5
text: Problem: Previously converted NAs 0, artificially introducing “cases” observations mislead model Solution: Linear interpolation within location preserves temporal trends Uses approx(method = "linear", rule = 1) interpolate interior NAs rule = 1 prevents extrapolation beyond data range (preserves boundaries) sets start/end NAs 0 interpolation impossible (surrounding data points) Applies independently location multi-location data Handles cases deaths time series Interior NAs interpolated: position 5 → 25 (20 30), position 8 → 45 (40 50) Boundary NAs set 0: positions 1-2 (first data), position 10 (last data) Impact: accurate representation missing data, better model training quality Reports number NAs interpolated vs. set 0 “Interpolated: X” shows successful linear interpolation “Set 0 (start/end): Y” shows boundary NAs Files modified: R/npe_posterior.R

title: MOSAIC 0.13.4
text: Problem: PyTorch silently propagates NaN/Inf values network, causing cryptic training failures Root cause: validation input data (X, y, weights) tensor conversion Impact: parameters observations contain NAs/Infs (corrupted files, invalid samples, bugs), propagate silently cause losses become NaN Fix: Add explicit validation checks X (parameters), y (observations), weights tensor conversion (line 170-266 npe.R) anyNA(X) (!.finite(X)) - parameters matrix anyNA(y) (!.finite(y)) - observations matrix anyNA(weights) (!.finite(weights)) - weight vector data structure failed (X, y, weights) Whether NAs Infs found Affected rows columns (first 5-10 shown) Likely sources corruption Specific solutions diagnose fix Catches data corruption training starts (saves time) Clear diagnostic messages pinpoint exact problem Prevents silent NaN propagation network Helps identify upstream bugs data preparation Files modified: R/npe.R Note: addresses user’s concern NA/NaN errors 25k evenly weighted samples (binary_retained) due numerical instability, rather data corruption

title: MOSAIC 0.13.3
text: Root cause: Validation loss became NA/NaN training, causing (val_loss < best_val_loss) comparison fail cryptic error Error: Error wrapup: missing value TRUE/FALSE needed followed recursive error abort Trigger: Low ESS (Kish: 1.2, Perplexity: 3.0) continuous_retained weight strategy causing numerical instability Fix: Add explicit NA/NaN checking train_loss val_loss early stopping comparison (line 384-405 npe.R) Impact: Now provides clear, actionable error message diagnostic information solutions epoch failed loss values Common causes (low ESS, weight concentration, architecture complexity) Specific solutions (try ‘continuous_best’, use ‘light’ tier, reduce learning rate) Files modified: R/npe.R Related: error masked recursive error handling - actual issue numerical instability degenerate weight distributions

title: MOSAIC 0.13.2
text: Root cause: Used jsonlite::fromJSON(..., simplifyVector = FALSE) loading config/priors disk, keeping JSON arrays R lists instead converting vectors Error: Even v0.13.1 fix get_npe_observed_data(), config$reported_cases still list, creating list columns data frames Fix: Replace jsonlite::fromJSON() read_json_to_list() (MOSAIC standard loader) 3 locations run_NPE.R (lines 244, 264, 284) Uses MOSAIC codebase standard JSON loading (consistent functions) Properly simplifies JSON arrays R vectors (simplifyVector = TRUE default) Supports gzipped JSON files maintainable consistent Impact: Resolves persistent CSV write errors run_NPE() standalone mode Files modified: R/run_NPE.R Verified: JSON loading, get_npe_observed_data(), CSV write succeed

title: MOSAIC 0.13.1

title: MOSAIC 0.10.25

title: MOSAIC 0.10.23

title: MOSAIC 0.10.10
text: Removed density-based weighting caused double-weighting artifact NPE samples drawn directly posterior p(θ|x), uniform weights correct Using density weights creating effective distribution [p(θ|x)]² -emphasized high-density regions -represented uncertainty Now uses uniform weights (NULL) proper posterior predictive sampling Fixed lines 1679-1686 run_MOSAIC.R Impact: NPE ensemble predictions now appropriate uncertainty bands Theory: posterior predictive p(y|x) ≈ (1/N) Σ p(y|θᵢ) θᵢ ~ p(θ|x) Density weights correct importance sampling q(θ) ≠ p(θ|x) See claude/npe_weighting_analysis.md complete theoretical analysis
